key_insight: Axion topological materials under static electric and magnetic fields can be described entirely through effective surface charge and current variables, reducing the problem to boundary integral equations.
engineering_relevance: This offers a boundary-based computational framework for predicting magnetoelectric response in topological insulators with complex geometries, useful for FEM-like surrogate modeling of heterogeneous systems.
method: Formulate the macroscopic quasi-static response in terms of surface charge and surface current densities.
Derive coupled integral equations governing the surface variables.
Solve analytically for axis-symmetric cases and numerically with a boundary finite element method.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports exact analytic solutions for axis-symmetric geometries and numerical demonstrations on spheres, hollow spheres, and toroidal surfaces.
watch_item: The boundary integral/BEM approach may become more difficult to implement and validate for strongly non-axisymmetric geometries or when resolving sharp geometric features.
paper_title: Surface Variables Description of Axion Topological Materials
